_comment: Damodaran FCFF inputs for NVIDIA Corporation (NVDA). Amounts in USD millions.
_data_source: FY2026 (ended Jan 26, 2026): SEC 10-K / 8-K filings, Q1 FY2027 balance sheet (April 2026). Revenue, EBIT, D&A from annual report; CapEx from GuruFocus/BusinessQuant; equity from Q1 FY2027 10-Q. Working capital change estimated from operating CF vs. net income.
_note: NVDA fiscal year ends January. FY2026 = Feb 2025 – Jan 2026. TTM anchored to FY2026; Q1 FY2027 annualizing at ~$340B run rate.
story_narrative: NVIDIA is the demand signal for the entire AI buildout. Q1 FY2027 results ($81.6B revenue, +85% YoY; Data Center $75B, +92% YoY) and Q2 FY2027 guidance of $91B confirm AI infrastructure investment is accelerating, not plateauing — Jensen Huang: 'demand has gone parabolic; agentic AI has arrived.' FY2026 revenue was $215.9B (8x in 3 years from FY2023's $27B). The CUDA ecosystem (15+ years of embedded developer tooling) makes switching costs structurally very high — AMD ROCm is the closest alternative but meaningfully behind. Blackwell architecture dominates training and is extending into inference. Vera Rubin (H2 FY2027) delivers 10x token cost reduction, sustaining the AI capex cycle well into 2028. Key risks: (1) hyperscaler custom ASIC substitution (Marvell/Broadcom) may take 15–25% of inference workloads by 2028–2029; (2) China export controls cap a historically significant revenue pool; (3) valuation at $5.3T embeds continued execution. DCF uses 22% 10-year CAGR from FY2026's $215.9B base — conservative given FY2027 is already tracking ~60% YoY, but appropriately reflects law-of-large-numbers reversion. Hyperscaler capex at $725B in 2026 (+77% YoY) directly underpins NVDA demand.
story_revenue_cagr: 22% CAGR over 10 years from FY2026 base ($215.9B). Actual FY2027 run rate (~60% YoY) will revert toward this as base grows. Bull case: 30%+ if Vera Rubin and agentic AI extend the current acceleration; bear case: 12–15% if custom ASICs take share.
story_target_margin: 55% EBIT margin reflects NVIDIA's extraordinary pricing power and software CUDA moat. FY2026 reported EBIT margin was ~63.6%; some normalization expected as competition in inference grows and R&D spend rises for Rubin/next-gen. 55% is a 10-year average target.
story_growth_years: 10 years covers Blackwell → Vera Rubin → next GPU architecture cycle. Each major architecture ~2 years; 10 years = ~5 GPU generations. Jensen's $1T Blackwell+Rubin order book provides near-term visibility.
story_stable_growth: 3% — in mature state NVIDIA grows with data center capex plus expansion into automotive/robotics. Slower than current but well above GDP given structural AI infrastructure buildout.
story_stable_margin: 38% stable EBIT — as inference competition intensifies from custom ASICs and AMD closes the CUDA gap, pricing power moderates from peak. Still premium vs. mature fabless peers.
story_s2c_high: 3.0x — NVIDIA is fabless (manufactures at TSMC). Incremental revenue requires almost no physical capital — R&D and CUDA ecosystem investment are the primary reinvestment categories. High S2C is the source of extreme FCF generation ($96.7B FCF in FY2026).
story_s2c_stable: 2.5x — slight decrease as NVIDIA invests in DGX Cloud, NIM inference infrastructure, and automotive platforms that require more capital than pure software/GPU.
story_beta: 1.75 high-growth beta — NVDA is highly sensitive to AI capex cycles; a 10% cut in hyperscaler budgets would hit NVDA revenue disproportionately. Stable beta 1.10 as CUDA ecosystem and diversification reduce cyclicality over time.
story_rd_cap: 5-year amortization for CUDA/GPU architecture IP. FY2026 R&D of $18.5B was 8.6% of revenue, slightly below historical % — reflects operating leverage. Cumulative CUDA IP amortizes over 5 years.
story_uncertainty: High — extraordinary growth embeds continued AI capex expansion; any deceleration (custom ASICs, macro shock, China escalation) would significantly reprice. Wide margin of safety required for BUY signal.
